text.title: Go Concurrency Patterns
text.summary: Worker pool implementation, fan-out/fan-in pattern, context propagation in concurrent code.

rule.statement: Use the Pool struct with a buffered jobs channel and context-aware workers. Pool.Submit must select on both the jobs channel and ctx.Done() to avoid deadlock under cancellation.
rule.rationale: Blocking submit on a full buffer without ctx awareness deadlocks when the caller is cancelled. The select pattern drains cleanly.

rule.statement: Use runtime.NumCPU() for default worker count. Close the jobs channel after all jobs are sent; let workers drain naturally.
rule.rationale: Closing the channel signals workers to exit their range loop cleanly without an explicit stop signal.

rule.statement: Write a race-detector test for all concurrent code: go test -race ./...
rule.rationale: The race detector catches data races that tests without -race miss. Required before any concurrent code is considered done.
